boardLabel2.text: H                         I                        J                        K                        L                        M                       N

green7.title: Move
green7.cardText: <span style='font-size: 10px;color: black'>Remove any demon cards from hand. Then move 6 green.

pink1.cardText: Draw 3 cards from your draw pile.

pink2.cardText: Draw 2 and remove up to 2 cards.

pink3.cardText: Draw 2 cards from your draw pile.

pink4.cardText: Draw 1 and remove up to 1 card.

pink5.title: Take 1
pink5.cardText: Take any card and add to discard pile.

pink7.title: Move 2
pink7.cardText: Move up to 2 adjacent spaces. Draw a curse.

pink8.title: Move
pink8.cardText: <span style='font-size: 10px'>Move to adjacent empty demon or mountain space. Do not draw curse.

pink9.title: Cave +1
pink9.cardText: Take 1 cave tile from the supply.

pink10.title: <span style='font-size: 16px'>Cave +1 / -0
pink10.cardText: Take 1 cave tile. May use 1 tile without removing it.

pink11.title: Market
pink11.cardText: Take 1 market card and place on draw pile.

pink12.title: <span style='font-size: 19px'> Move 3x1
pink12.cardText: <span style='font-size: 10px'>Move 3 times on spaces with 1 symbol, including demons & barriers.

pink13.title: Move 6
pink13.cardText: <span style='font-size: 10px'>Move up to 6 spaces including barrier, occupied & mountain spaces.

pink14.title: Move 1
pink14.cardText: <span style='font-size: 10px'>Move 1 adjacent space including barrier, occupied & mountain spaces.

pink15.cardText: <span style='font-size: 10px'>Move opponents 1 adjacent empty space. No tunnels, barriers, or caves.

cave4.power: Move 1 gold space or use to buy a card.

cave5.power: Move 2 gold spaces or use to buy a card.

cave8.power: Draw 1 card from your draw pile. You can play it in the same turn.

cave9.power: Remove any single card in your hand from the game.

cave10.power: You can replace your hand cards. Place up to 4 cards from your hand above your hand area. Draw the same amount from your draw pile.

cave11.power: Use after playing a card that must be removed from the game. Put it in your discard pile instead of removing it.

cave12.power: Allows you to move onto or through an occupied space for the rest of your turn. No moving into mountains.

cave13.power: Move onto any adjacent space except mountains or occupied spaces.

cave14.power: Change the symbol/color of the next card you play.

cave15.power: Move a demon card from your hand to another player's discard pile.

cave16.power: When you draw a curse, look at it and decide if you want to cancel it. Do so by removing both the curse and this tile.

cave17.power: Move onto any adjacent empty demon or mountain space. Do not draw a curse. Does not work on cave spaces.

cave18.power: Buy any off-market card. Leave remaining cards from that pile in place.

cave19.power: Play this as if it were one of the indigenous cards of an opponent. The opponent's card is not affected.

cave20.power: Play this as if it were one of your indigenous cards. Your card is not affected.

nameArray0: Over the River and Through the Woods
Buckle Up. It's Gonna Be a Long Ride.
Don't Get Lost in the Woods!
The Shortest is Sometimes the Longest

instructions1.html: To begin, make sure all players are seated. Then press the restart button and select expansion options. There is also an option if you want to be able to edit a board.

instructions2.html: Click the number to choose a preset board layout. If editing maps, delete tiles by dropping them in the lower right. Add tiles by double clicking on the board. In the bottom right are also options for showing the blocks, saving and deleting maps, and changing scale.

instructions3.html: Only some boards have barriers added. For the others, manually drag barriers to desired positions. When the board is set up the way you want, click the lock button to randomize the barrier data and complete other setup functions. After starting the game you can lock and unlock the board tiles in case you need to move them without impacting anything else.

curse1.power: Draw a demon card and put it on your discard pile.

curse2.power: The next player moves your piece that activated the curse 2 adjacent empty spaces. No tunnels, barriers, or cave tokens.

curse3.power: In turn order, each other player moves their own piece 1 adjacent empty space. No tunnels, barriers, or cave tokens.

curse4.power: Do not play this symbol until you discard at least as many symbols as indicated on the top line of the card.

curse5.power: Do not play this symbol until you discard at least as many symbols as indicated on the top line of the card.

curse6.power: Do not play this symbol until you discard at least as many symbols as indicated on the top line of the card.

curse7.power: Do not play pink cards until you discard one without using it.

curse8.power: You may collect, but cannot play, cave tokens until you remove one without using it.

curse9.power: Do not play any cards until you have discarded 2. If you only have 1, you cannot lift the curse this turn but you may discard/keep it as usual.

After clicking OK, you have the specified number of seconds (default is 10) to take a screenshot using software on your device. To make the best image for the board selection menu, capture or crop the area just inside the rectangle. Save the screenshot to your device and upload it using the Save icon button to the right.

fields.label: Select number of seconds for screenshot

map.detail:
Creating/Editing
Scale and hex grids limit the way maps can be made. The most important thing to remember is that you should not move the board and you shoud not add map tiles to the board unless the scale is = 1. The entire board background is red as a reminder when the scale is not 1.To set scale equal to one, click once on (or drag to the middle) the small white button in the slider. Once the map is fully created, you can use the scale button and drag the entire board around (by clicking and dragging anywhere in the play area not on a tile) to get the best fit within the play area.

If you start with an existing map and the scale is not 1, set the scale to 1 and then move each tile (and barrier) slightly to find the new place the grid aligns with. Then you can add new tiles.

To add new tiles, double click any where in the play area, select the expansion, and then select an available tile (or multiple tiles). The tiles will be added to the location where you double clicked and then you drag them from there. Click a tile to rotate it.

You can optionally add the barriers. They do not snap into location to allow maximum flexibility. Drag them by the icon in their middle to avoid offset effects when dropping them. The barriers are randomized at the start of play. There is no way to force specific barriers into specific locations.
Saving
Saving a game only saves it to the room in its current state. If you want the save to persistent once you load another VTT module, then you must save the game to your room. See the wiki for instructions (click on the question mark icon in Edit Mode) if you do not know how to do that.

Before saving the game, consider making a screenshot. If you do not add a screenshot, when selecting maps you will only get the name and number of the map. To take a screenshot, get your screen capturing software ready. Then press the red and white PNG icon button and follow the prompt. Save the screenshot to your device.

To save the map, press the red and white save icon and follow the prompts. You can overwrite an existing game (except 0, 100, 200, 300) or create a new one. You can upload a screenshot image or not. And you must give the game a name (unless overwriting and keeping the same name).

You can also delete a game (except the blank ones) using the red and white trash icon. To do so, follow the prompts after pressing that button.

base.detail:
Base Game

Info
  1. This is your personal hand. It is only visible to you. This is the only area on the board that has limited visibility. It will automatically refill up to 4 cards at the end of your turn. The number of cards in each players' hand is shown in the number next to the player's name on the seat.
  2. This is the play area for the player whose turn it is. Move cards from your hand to here to play them. The color helps indicate the active player.
  3. This is the draw pile for the player whose turn it is. When it is your turn, cards automatically move from #4 in your seat area to here. If you get to draw a card(s) during your turn drag them from here to your hand. If your draw pile is empty, press the black and white "Get Discards & Shuffle" button to refill the draw pile from the discard pile.
  4. This is where draw cards are held for players awaiting their turn.
  5. This is the discard pile for the player whose turn it is. Put any cards here that you do not want in your hand, but that are not being removed from the game. Cards in the play area will automatically be moved here at the end of your turn.
  6. This is where the discard cards are held for players awaiting their turn.
  7. Store cave tokens here until they are used.
  8. Put barriers that you collected here for scoring in case of a tie.
  9. The button above the 9 is the turn button. Press it at the end of your turn to automate card movement. The seat area of the player whose turn it is will be emphasized. The initial starting player has a blue pawn next to their name. The button below the 9 will briefly emphasize the location of the player tokens. The arrow is movable and can be dragged around the room as a demonstration aid.
  10. The cards at the top of the screen are off market.
  11. You should not need these buttons during gameplay. They are mainly for setting up the board. However, you could use the lock/unlock button to move the map around or the show/hide market button to hide the cards.
  12. The cards on the right are on market.
  13. Move to these holders any cave tokens and cards (and curse tokens in one of the expansions) that are being removed from play and no longer available for use by players.

exp1.detail:
Expansion 1 (Similar to Heroes & Hexes)
Info
  1. These are the familiar cards. In turn, take one and flip it over so everyone can see it. Add to your draw pile. The draw pile is the stack of 8 cards near your seat (or on the left near the hand if you are the first player). Everyone then clicks on the pile handle to shuffle the cards. Manually draw 4 cards to your hand the first time you play.
  2. These are the demon cards. Manually drag one to your discard pile when required by the rules.
  3. The top pile of circular tokens are the curses. Drag one and flip it over. Hover over it for a text explanation of the symbols. Curses with a red border are triggered immediately and removed from the game by dropping them in the red circle on the bottom right of the board. Place curses with a black border anywhere near your seat until the curse ends. The lower pile of hexagonal tokens are extra cave tokens that were not distributed on the board. Drag and flip those just like the curses or other cave tokens.
  4. The bottom row of cards are off market cards. They are treated the same as the top row. They are simply located here for space considerations.
  5. These are the hero cards. When eligible to pick a hero, drag 3 to anywhere on the board and flip them over. Choose one and put it into your hand. The other 2 go back into the pile. They will be turned face down automatically. Press the "Shuffle" button to get the cards ready for the next player.

exp2.detail:
Expansion 2 (Similar to Dangers & Muisca)
Info
  1. The colored squares represent the 4 artifacts available when visiting shrines. When eligible to claim an artifact, click the appropriate colored square to add a check mark. Shrines on the map are represented by 3 hexes of the same type surrounded by a black border with a red and white "x" square centered in the middle of the 3 hexes.
  2. The indigenous cards (similar to Muisca) optionally can be stored above the play area for later use (by you or an opponent in some cases). These colored holders will keep up to 2 cards available.
  3. Two indigenous cards are always available on the market on the right side of the board until the cards run out.

info.name: Temple Trek
info.attribution:
Room layout, card and tile design, coding, and library image by LawDawg and released to the Public Domain under CC0. Seat area layout design and coding by Jonathan Cox, released to the public domain under CC0.

Board map layouts:
  • Maps 1-7 and 201-206 from the setup guide in the rules.
  • Maps 8-30 by tom tom (1qwerty) from https://boardgamegeek.com/filepage/172663/40-custom-maps-for-the-quest-for-el-dorado, used with permission, and released to the Public Domain under CC0.
  • Maps 101-106 by cosminul (based on 1querty's maps) from https://boardgamegeek.com/thread/2956366/maps-needed/page/1, used with permission, and released to the Public Domain under CC0.
  • Maps 207-209 and 301-305 by Rob1012 from https://boardgamegeek.com/filepage/251675/14-maps-for-dangers-and-muisca-expansion, used with permission, and released to the Public Domain under CC0.
Available under the Pixabay license:
  • Wood grain background by RyanPro, https://pixabay.com/photos/trees-wood-yellow-wood-oak-1915247/
  • Mayan pyramid by sinisamaric1, https://pixabay.com/vectors/mayan-travel-temple-pyramid-mexico-4454152/
  • Sound while changing turns is "Wrong Answer" by UNIVERSFIELD, https://pixabay.com/sound-effects/wrong-answer-129254/.
Icons on the cards and tiles have attribution as listed in the About overlay.
info.description: Buy and discard cards to build the best deck to help your explorer find the lost temple.

info.helpText:
This module contains options to use the expansions similar to "Heroes and Hexes", and "Dangers and Muisca", or both in combination. It also includes 54 pre-built maps and has tools to help you build your own.

Warning: Do not use the VTT History undo function. It does not work well with this game and will probably break it.

Warning: If using a Safari browser, it may take up to 1 minute to load the room.

Players take seats. Additional instructions are in the room and available in player aids in the bottom center of the screen.
